description_long: This object is a retro-style computer monitor with a thick, boxy frame and a prominent square display. The screen is displaying a blue error message, reminiscent of the classic Windows 'Blue Screen of Death.' The monitor is encased in a matte black housing with a wide, flat base that appears to support the weight of the unit. A circular, white disc is partially visible at the front base, suggesting the presence of a built-in CD or DVD drive. The sides and rear of the monitor are solid and angular, with no visible ports or ventilation grilles. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a robust, industrial appearance.
description: A retro, boxy black computer monitor with a square screen displaying a blue error message and a built-in disc drive at the front base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square screen with a blue error message and a white disc partially inserted at the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep, angular housing and the protruding rear section of the monitor.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, flat back panel with no visible features or ports.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the monitor's depth and the angular design of the housing.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and matte black, with sharp corners and no visible decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, flat rectangular base integrated into the housing, made of the same matte black plastic as the rest of the unit, with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: A few small buttons are visible on the lower front panel, just below the screen, grouped together near the right side.
